stories.title: F1 2027 Engine Direction Shift — All Stakeholders Agree to Boost ICE Power, Reduce ERS

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
BREAKING: F1 is shifting away from electric power in 2027.

ICE up ~50kW. ERS down ~50kW.
All 5 PU manufacturers agreed.

Less super clipping. More racing. This is huge. #F1

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The 2026 F1 regs require drivers to "harvest energy" more than "race."

In 2027, that changes. Less ERS, more combustion — unanimously agreed by every team and manufacturer.

Real racing is coming back. #F1

article.headline: F1 2027 Engine Revolution — All Stakeholders Agree to Boost ICE Power and Reduce ERS

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
F1 2027 Engine Direction Shift

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Search terms:** FIA Formula 1 team principals
- **Canva overlay text:** "2027 F1: ICE UP 50kW | ERS DOWN 50kW — ALL STAKEHOLDERS AGREE"
- **Mood:** Decisive, industry-changing, authoritative
- **Status:** not_started

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Search terms:** Formula 1 power unit engine
- **Canva overlay text:** "F1 SHIFTS AWAY FROM ELECTRIC POWER IN 2027"
- **Mood:** Technical, landmark decision
- **Status:** not_started

---

research.facts:
F1 2027 Engine Direction Shift
**Sources consulted:** FIA.com announcement, Formula1.com, Sky Sports, Motorsport.com, Yahoo Sports
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- FIA, all team principals, FOM, and all 5 PU manufacturers agreed in principle to 2027 rule changes [HIGH — FIA official announcement]
- ICE power will increase by approximately 50kW [HIGH — FIA official announcement]
- ERS deployment power will reduce by approximately 50kW [HIGH — FIA official announcement]
- Goal: reduce super clipping, lifting and coasting — making driving more natural [HIGH — FIA statement]
- Current 2026 regulations have near 50:50 ICE/ERS split [HIGH — multiple sources]
- 2027 changes shift to approximately 60:40 ICE/ERS [HIGH — Motorsport.com]
- Still requires WMSC rubber stamp + technical group discussion [HIGH — FIA statement]
**Story angle:** Unanimous consensus on walking back the electric power emphasis — a direct response to driver criticism of unnatural driving styles in 2026.

stories.title: Honda Gets a Lifeline — ADUO Rule Change Gives Aston Martin's Engine Partner Critical R&D Boost

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Aston Martin just got a massive lifeline.

FIA's ADUO rule gives Honda 40 extra R&D hours and an $11M cost cap reduction. That's the kind of help a last-place team needs.

Now Newey just needs to get back trackside. #F1

article.headline: Honda Gets an FIA Lifeline — Can Aston Martin's ADUO Development Boost Change Their 2026?

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Honda/Aston Martin ADUO Lifeline

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Search terms:** Adrian Newey Aston Martin
- **Canva overlay text:** "ASTON MARTIN GETS $19M LIFELINE — HONDA GRANTED R&D BOOST"

- **Status:** not_started

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Search terms:** Aston Martin Formula 1 car
- **Canva overlay text:** "ADUO RULE CHANGE: HONDA GETS 40 EXTRA R&D HOURS"
- **Mood:** Technical development, recovery
- **Status:** not_started

---

research.facts:
Honda/Aston Martin ADUO Lifeline
**Sources consulted:** GPFans.com, PlanetF1.com, Motorsport.com, MotorBiscuit, SundayGuardianLive
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- FIA's ADUO system expanded with new tier: more than 10% behind best performer gets extra allocation [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Honda granted 230 hours of test bench time (up from 190) [HIGH — GPFans, sundayguardianlive]
- $11 million cost cap downward adjustment approved [HIGH — GPFans headline]
- $8 million one-off bonus for inaugural 2026 season [HIGH — MotorBiscuit]
- Aston Martin last in constructors' championship [HIGH — from championship data]
- Newey hospitalized (illness reported as pneumonia); working from home [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Aston Martin confirmed "Adrian is working and was on campus last week" [HIGH — Aston Martin statement]
- Andy Cowell is team principal [HIGH — from previous research]
**Story angle:** Honda's R&D lifeline could change Aston Martin's trajectory — but Newey needs to be trackside to implement what he's designing.

stories.title: Canadian GP Preview — Sprint Weekend Debut, Antonelli Eyes Fourth Straight Win

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
2 weeks until Canada. 🇨🇦

Circuit Gilles Villeneuve. Sprint weekend. ~34 pts available.
Antonelli leads by 49 over Norris.

First sprint of 2026. McLaren and Mercedes both bringing upgrades. #F1 #CanadianGP

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
The Canadian GP is a power-sensitive, low-drag circuit.

McLaren's setup should suit it perfectly. Mercedes is bringing a big aero package too.

Antonelli has won every race this season. But can he win in Montreal? #F1 #CanadianGP

research.facts:
Canadian GP Preview
**Sources consulted:** Formula1.com, GPFans, Motorsport.com, Montreal Grand Prix website
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Canadian Grand Prix: May 22-24, 2026 [HIGH — Formula1.com official timetable]
- Circuit Gilles Villeneuve, Montreal [HIGH — official site]
- First sprint weekend of the 2026 season [HIGH — multiple sources]
- 70-lap race over 4.361-km circuit [HIGH — Formula1.com]
- Race starts Sunday May 24 at 16:00 local time (ET) [HIGH — Formula1.com]
- McLaren bringing upgrade package to Canada [HIGH — Sky Sports]
- Mercedes targeting major aero upgrade for Canada [HIGH — Sky Sports]
- Ferrari may delay next major package [HIGH — Sky Sports]
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- ~34 total pts available at sprint weekend (8 sprint + 26 race max) [MEDIUM — calculated from F1 scoring]
**Story angle:** Canada's sprint format makes it the most points-dense single event of 2026 so far — maximum pressure on championship rivals to deliver.

stories.title: Championship Math — Can Norris and Hamilton Close the Gap at Canada's Sprint Weekend?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Antonelli leads Norris by 49 points.

Canada is a sprint weekend — 34 pts available in one weekend.

If Norris sweeps the sprint + race and Antonelli scores nothing, the gap goes from 49 to 15.

Canada matters. A LOT. #F1

article.headline: Championship Math — Why Canada's Sprint Weekend Is Norris and Hamilton's Best Chance to Make This a Title Fight

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Championship Math

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)

- **Status:** not_started

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)

- **Mood:** Analysis, championship math

---

research.facts:
Championship Math at Canada Sprint
**Sources consulted:** Championship standings (after Miami); F1 calendar
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Antonelli: 100 pts; Russell: 80 pts; Leclerc: 59 pts; Norris: 51 pts; Hamilton: 51 pts; Verstappen: 26 pts [HIGH — multiple standings sources post-Miami]
- Sprint maximum points: 8 (winner); Race maximum: 25 (winner) + 1 (fastest lap) [HIGH — F1 official rules]
- McLaren's low-drag setup suits Circuit Gilles Villeneuve [HIGH — multiple technical sources]
- Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a low-drag, power-sensitive street circuit [HIGH — circuit characteristics]
**Story angle:** The sprint format gives Norris and Hamilton double the chances to compress the championship — if Antonelli has any off weekend, the title picture changes quickly.

stories.title: Verstappen's Summer Deadline — Exit Clause Window Narrows After Miami

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen is 74 points behind Antonelli, sitting 7th in the championship.

His reported exit clause requires a top-3 position at summer break.

Canada's sprint format gives him a chance to score extra points — but he needs podiums, not just points. #F1

article.headline: Verstappen's Exit Clause — The Summer Deadline Is Approaching and the Numbers Don't Lie

- **Search terms:** Max Verstappen Red Bull Racing

research.facts:
Verstappen Summer Exit Clause
**Sources consulted:** Previous pipeline research (highly confirmed); GPFans; motorsport.com
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Verstappen 7th in championship, 26 pts, 74 behind Antonelli after 4 races [HIGH]
- Red Bull Miami upgrade halved weight deficit (~30kg to ~15kg over 768kg minimum) [HIGH — multiple sources including McLaren's Stella commenting on it]
- Exit clause widely reported: must be top-3 at summer break [MEDIUM — widely reported, not officially confirmed by Red Bull]
- Full weight/chassis solution expected for summer 2026 [HIGH — reported by multiple technical sources]
**Story angle:** Verstappen needs podiums in Canada's sprint format to realistically close the 74-point gap before the summer deadline.
